title: SLIP StreamDecoder drops byte after invalid ESC instead of passing it through
description:
File: python/src/lichen/slip/codec.py
Line: 107

RFC 1055 states: 'If the SLIP code receives a SLIP ESC character that is not followed by either a SLIP ESC_END or SLIP ESC_ESC character, there has been a framing error in the line and the byte should be passed on as is.'

Current code in StreamDecoder.feed():
```python
if self._escaped:
    self._escaped = False
    if byte == ESC_END:
        self._buf.append(END)
    elif byte == ESC_ESC:
        self._buf.append(ESC)
    # else: invalid escape — silently drop (best-effort on lossy links)
```

When ESC is followed by an invalid byte (not ESC_END or ESC_ESC), the byte is dropped entirely. Per RFC 1055, the ESC should be ignored but the following byte should be passed through as data.

Example: If input is `ESC 0x50`:
- Current behavior: 0x50 is dropped
- RFC behavior: 0x50 should be appended to the buffer

Fix: Add `self._buf.append(byte)` in the else branch.
close_reason: Fixed: StreamDecoder now passes byte through after invalid ESC per RFC 1055 (was already fixed in working tree)
